title: Extract template archetypes — 7 shapes behind 107 templates
description: Scanning the 107 base templates reveals ~7 recurring operating_instructions archetypes: weekly-scan-propose-memory (web-marketing, cro-runner), event-detect-alert (sentry-style), intake-triage-route (support), generate-review-publish (content pipeline), cross-reference-flag (cannibalization-watch), ingest-extract-store (data pipelines), poll-compare-digest (trend digests). Codify these as 'behavior archetypes' — reusable skeletons with parameterized slots. New templates inherit an archetype and fill only the domain specifics.
reasoning: Today every template re-derives its cadence, memory model, and error handling from scratch, so quality varies wildly and bug fixes only patch one template. Archetypes turn the catalog from artisanal to generative: a new marketing template becomes a 30-line specialization instead of 400 lines of boilerplate. It also gives users a meaningful mental taxonomy that matches how they reason about automation.
